title: Venezuelan Buckwheat Pilaf
description: A Chinese-inspired dish that combines the earthy flavors of buckwheat and venezuelan ingredients with a rustic twist.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of aerated vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Add the buckwheat groats and venezuelan rice to the skillet, stirring to combine with the onion and garlic mixture.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the buckwheat and rice are lightly toasted.
Add the soy sauce and sesame oil to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed.
Transfer the pilaf to a serving dish and garnish with chopped scallions and peanuts.
